deep_dive.title: Simulation Based Reward Function Validation for Multi-Agent On Orbit Inspection
deep_dive.abstract: A proposed method for the control of groups of inspection spacecraft is Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). While MARL has already been employed for this purpose in previous work, the reward functions used focus on reaching a finite set of predetermined inspection points around the target. In this work, we study and develop a generalized reward function for the MARL inspection task informed by the analysis of 3D reconstructions of inspected objects in orbit. Because the reward function is generalized such that any number of images at arbitrary locations may evaluated, we also allow trained agents to have complete control over when images are collected. With this approach, we gather insights into best practices for not only the specific MARL inspection task, but also gain key takeaways informative to the broader inspection task outside of a MARL context.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning applications for on-orbit inspection

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Develops a generalized reward function for MARL-based on-orbit inspection, enabling agents to control image collection timing and improving flexibility.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对在轨检查任务中的多智能体强化学习奖励函数设计展开研究。现有方法通常要求智能体到达预定的检查点，限制了灵活性。作者基于对在轨物体三维重建的分析，提出了一种泛化奖励函数，允许智能体自主决定何时采集图像。实验表明该方法能提升任务效率，并为未来航天器群体控制提供了最佳实践。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: cooperative multi agent reinforcement learning applications

deep_dive.title: Mean Field Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: This monograph provides an introduction to mean field reinforcement learning through the lens of Markov decision processes arising from large-population stochastic control with mean field interactions and common noise. Starting from the connection between multi-agent reinforcement learning and mean field control, it develops the probabilistic, mathematical, and control-theoretic framework needed to formulate representative-agent learning problems, analyze their relationship with finite-population systems, and study both general and linear-quadratic models. The presentation includes dynamic programming principles, propagation-of-chaos limits, and theoretical analyses of tabular Q-learning and policy-gradient methods. It also discusses numerical implementations, including tabular schemes and deep reinforcement learning methods such as deep deterministic policy gradient. The goal is to give readers a coherent bridge between mean field control theory and reinforcement learning methodology, emphasizing the mathematical structure of the problems and the design of tractable learning approaches for large stochastic populations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: monograph on mean field reinforcement learning from MARL perspective
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从多智能体强化学习角度介绍平均场强化学习的专著

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Provides a comprehensive introduction to mean field RL, connecting multi-agent RL to mean field control, with DP principles, theoretical analysis, and numerical implementations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该专著从多智能体强化学习与平均场控制的联系出发，系统介绍了平均场强化学习的概率论、数学和控制论框架。书中详细阐述了代表性智能体学习问题、有限种群系统的关系、动态规划原理、传播混沌极限，以及表格Q学习和策略梯度方法的理论分析，并讨论了包括表格方案在内的数值实现。为大规模多智能体系统的学习提供了坚实的理论基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该专著从多智能体强化学习与平均场控制的联系出发，系统介绍了平均场强化学习的概率论、数学和控制论框架。书中详细阐述了代表性智能体学习问题、有限种群系统的关系、动态规划原理、传播混沌极限，以及表格Q学习和策略梯度方法的理论分析，并讨论了包括表格方案在内的数值实现。为大规模多智能体系统的学习提供了坚实的理论基础。

deep_dive.title: Towards Value-Constrained Credit Assignment in Fully Delegated AI Cooperatives
deep_dive.abstract: We propose a framework for reward allocation in fully delegated AI cooperatives where humans are represented by agents that contribute data and participate in model updates under heterogeneous value constraints. The key idea is to credit only those updates that remain admissible after screening them against each principal's value profile. We formulate value-conditioned gradient filtering, online marginal contribution signals, and cumulative revenue settlement within a traversal learning (TL) substrate. TL is especially attractive here because it performs decentralized backpropagation without the quality loss associated with aggregation-centric distributed learning and, we argue, offers a finer attribution substrate than FedAvg-style federated learning by preserving explicit traversal and gradient paths. The framework is positioned against data valuation, federated contribution estimation, personalized federated learning, and pluralistic alignment.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reward allocation in cooperative AI systems with credit assignment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 协作人工智能系统中的奖励分配与信用分配
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 协作人工智能系统中的奖励分配与信用分配
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 协作人工智能系统中的奖励分配与信用分配
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes value-constrained credit assignment for cooperative multi-agent AI systems using traversal learning, enabling fine-grained attribution and alignment.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对完全委托的AI合作社中奖励分配问题，提出基于价值约束的信用分配框架，通过遍历学习实现去中心化反向传播，同时进行价值条件梯度过滤和在线边际贡献信号结算。该方法在保持分布式优势的同时提供了更细粒度的归因，适用于协作多智能体强化学习场景。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对完全委托的AI合作社中奖励分配问题，提出基于价值约束的信用分配框架，通过遍历学习实现去中心化反向传播，同时进行价值条件梯度过滤和在线边际贡献信号结算。该方法在保持分布式优势的同时提供了更细粒度的归因，适用于协作多智能体强化学习场景。

deep_dive.title: Controllable Sim Agents with Behavior Latents
deep_dive.abstract: Realistic traffic simulation requires agents that imitate logged behavior and can also be steered along interpretable axes. Such controllability enables engineers to isolate variables, reproduce specific edge cases, and test autonomous systems without real-world risk. We introduce Controllable Neural Variational Agents (CNeVA), a controllable simulated-agent framework that learns to infer a per-agent Gaussian behavior latent from per-channel discounted returns via a closed-form conjugate variational update, conditioning a rectified-flow trajectory generator trained on a mixed channel-mask curriculum for classifier-free guidance. To tackle scarcity in reward signals, we propose soft eligibility gates that replace hard binary thresholds with smooth exponential decay, preserving the gradient signal for near-threshold agents. On the Waymo Open Motion Dataset, CNeVA attains competitive realism on the benchmark while exposing per-channel controllability that the higher-ranked imitation models lack. Speed- and acceleration-based steering produces monotone responses without stall-induced reward hacking. Safety controllability is monotone and substantial with the introduction of soft eligibility. We manage to achieve steerable map compliance under a context-residual return measure. Furthermore, our experiment demonstrates that steering metrics must be read alongside physical-plausibility guardrails to avoid reward-hacking confounds.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: decentralized agent behavior latents for traffic simulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于交通仿真的去中心化智能体行为隐变量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于交通仿真的去中心化智能体行为隐变量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于交通仿真的去中心化智能体行为隐变量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CNeVA for controllable multi-agent traffic simulation with per-agent behavior latents and closed-form variational updates, supporting decentralized execution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对交通仿真中多智能体可控性需求，提出CNeVA框架，为每个智能体学习高斯行为隐变量，通过闭式共轭变分更新和整流流生成器生成轨迹。软资格门限解决了奖励稀疏问题。在多个场景中实现了高保真且可控的仿真，为多智能体强化学习中的去中心化执行提供了可行方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对交通仿真中多智能体可控性需求，提出CNeVA框架，为每个智能体学习高斯行为隐变量，通过闭式共轭变分更新和整流流生成器生成轨迹。软资格门限解决了奖励稀疏问题。在多个场景中实现了高保真且可控的仿真，为多智能体强化学习中的去中心化执行提供了可行方案。

quick_skim.title: When Multi-Robot Systems Meet Agentic AI:Towards Embodied Collective Intelligence
quick_skim.abstract: Embodied AI is increasingly becoming agentic, shifting robots from perception--control pipelines towards closed-loop systems that can retrieve context, deliberate during execution, monitor feedback, and refine future behavior. In parallel, robotics research has also moved from single-robot autonomy towards multi-robot systems, driven by the need for wider sensing, distributed action, heterogeneous capabilities, and fault tolerance. As AI agents move from single-agent use towards multi-agent collaboration, robotics faces a parallel challenge: robot teams must move beyond sharing maps, task assignments, and datasets towards sharing the state produced by embodied agent loops. This article explores Embodied Collective Intelligence (ECI), a future multi-robot paradigm in which a robot team accumulates and uses world context, task progress, and skill experience as shared resources. Specifically, we first review how embodied AI is becoming agentic and how multi-robot cooperation has evolved. We then present Embodied Collective Intelligence through Co-Perception, Co-Action, and Co-Evolution. Finally, we use an illustrative navigation study to examine one concrete component of the concept: shared world-memory inheritance. The study shows that a newly added robot can benefit from merged team memory, but it is not intended as a full evaluation of the ECI framework. Taken together, the review and conceptual framework motivate Embodied Collective Intelligence as a direction for embodied multi-agent intelligence, while the case study grounds one measurable part of the concept.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多机器人系统与多智能体协作
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多机器人系统与多智能体协作
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多机器人系统与多智能体协作
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Explores the parallel from single to multi-robot and single to multi-agent, proposing Embodied Collective Intelligence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文探讨了具身AI从感知-控制闭环向智能体化发展以及机器人从单机向多机器人系统演进的并行趋势。随着AI智能体从单智能体走向多智能体协作，机器人团队需共享由具身智能体循环产生的状态。文章提出了具身集体智能（ECI）的未来愿景，为多智能体强化学习社区提供了重要视角。
